summary: Establish the M1 C12 schema-and-gates slice with complete shipped theme tokens, strict custom-theme schema parsing, a conservative WCAG contrast oracle, and strengthened hardcoded-color enforcement.
specSections: §2.7 questions, decisions, and unit of work

externalInputs.purpose: Confirm the WCAG 2.2 normal-text 4.5:1 threshold, unrounded threshold comparison, and contrast-ratio definition.
Confirm the WCAG 2.2 non-text control and focus-boundary contrast basis used by the conservative matrix.
Confirm that Three.js material specular values are colors and constructor color properties accept Color.set representations for source-policy coverage.
Confirm light-dark() and related CSS Color Level 5 functions for component color-literal gate coverage.

decisions.decision: Store each shipped theme as an individual Appendix C JSON file and validate exact keys, nesting, metadata values, and string token types at runtime.
decisions.rationale: A single normative artifact per theme prevents partial token sets and makes completeness independently testable without relying on TypeScript import inference.

decisions.decision: Use opaque six-digit sRGB hex for every shipped token while keeping the custom-theme parser's color validator injectable.
decisions.rationale: Opaque sRGB yields deterministic luminance arithmetic for shipped themes. Appendix C's broader custom CSS-color meaning remains parked under Q-0006 rather than silently narrowed.

decisions.decision: Until Q-0005 is amended, audit 88 explicit foreground/background relationships at 4.5:1 for readable text and 3:1 for non-text controls, focus indicators, and viewer marks; compare unrounded ratios.
decisions.rationale: The explicit conservative matrix is reviewable and deterministic while avoiding an unsupported claim that the owner has finalized the normative pairing policy.

decisions.decision: Audit editor.text against editor.selection and require the runtime slice to normalize selected syntax to editor.text; audit syntax tokens against normal and active-line backgrounds.
decisions.rationale: Appendix C has no selection-specific syntax palette. A single selected-text foreground gives the defined tokens a testable contrast relationship without inventing schema keys.

decisions.decision: Deliver exact JSON parsing plus structured schema and injected color diagnostics now; park the settings-facing import control under Q-0004 and make no end-to-end FR-12.4 claim.
decisions.rationale: The parser is independent of settings UI, while the milestone ownership of the visible import control is genuinely ambiguous.

decisions.decision: Scan product JavaScript, TypeScript, JSX, TSX, and CSS outside the canonical token files; use the TypeScript lexer for JS/TS comments and a string-aware CSS comment scanner; reject non-fragment hex broadly while requiring semantic color contexts for ambiguous named and numeric forms.
decisions.rationale: The wider scan closes root-file, gradient, CSS fallback, SVG, canvas, and Three.js numeric bypasses without treating identifiers, URL fragments, selectors, or custom-property names as palette literals.

testEvidence.criterion: AC-12.a and Appendix C exact schema
testEvidence.test: The runtime guard accepts only the complete normative object and rejects every missing required path, unknown key, wrong metadata value, wrong token type, and non-object root.

red.outcome: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 97 failed | 4 passed (101). The deliberately incomplete guard returned true for missing, extra, wrong-typed, and non-object cases.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-12.1 shipped themes and AC-12.a completeness
testEvidence.test: Exactly one complete, uniquely named Light, Dark, and High Contrast theme ships, and every color leaf is opaque six-digit sRGB.

red.outcome: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed | 1 passed (2). The empty shipped-theme stub had length zero instead of three.

green.outcome: Retained GREEN transcript reviewed at ledger time; original start time was not retained. Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 2 passed (2).

testEvidence.test: Custom-theme parsing returns a typed theme or ordered structured issues for malformed JSON, exact-schema failure, and each rejected color token.

red.outcome: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 4 failed (4). The initial stub always returned an empty failure result.

green.outcome: Retained GREEN transcript reviewed at ledger time; original start time was not retained. Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 4 passed (4).

testEvidence.test: The exact WCAG sRGB oracle audits all 88 declared pairs, all shipped themes pass, and a deliberately collapsed foreground produces the expected 1:1 failure.

red.outcome: Retained RED transcripts reviewed at ledger time. The initial oracle stub produced 2 failed | 3 passed tests. After the broader pair assertions were added, 2 tests failed for missing pairs; the first complete matrix then produced 3 failed | 4 passed tests and listed six Dark, seven Light, and three High Contrast palette failures. A controlled `auditThemeContrast` no-op mutation later made the negative-oracle test fail at 2026-07-10T00:03:17-06:00.

green.outcome: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 8 passed (8), including all three shipped palettes and the negative-oracle falsification case.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-12.2, AC-12.b, and V-2 component color gate
testEvidence.test: The source scanner rejects literal colors across product source and distinguishes comments, fragments, selectors, URLs, identifiers, and token references.

red.outcome: Retained full-suite RED transcripts reviewed at ledger time. Successive adversarial cycles produced 17 failed | 15 passed (32), 5 failed | 32 passed (37), 8 failed | 38 passed (46), 9 failed | 46 passed (55), and finally 2 failed | 55 passed (57). Failures covered root and generated paths, short and separated numeric Three.js forms, light/fog/material/color methods, gradients, multiline and CSS Color Level 5 functions, color-mix, fallbacks, React camel-case properties, CSS custom properties and shorthands, system colors, SVG/canvas, CSS comment boundaries, and fragment/token false positives.

green.command: cmd /c "pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/source-policy.test.ts --reporter=dot && pnpm.cmd lint"
green.outcome: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 57 passed (57); duration 948ms. Biome checked 50 files with no fixes, and the repository source-policy scan passed.

testEvidence.test: Only the canonical token stylesheet is exempt from literal-color checks; generated, fixture, theme-component, and nested theme-directory source remains covered.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/source-policy.test.ts -t "applies color policy" --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed | 45 skipped (46). The nested src/theme/themes/ThemePreview.tsx fixture was incorrectly exempt.

green.command: cmd /c "pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/source-policy.test.ts --reporter=dot && pnpm.cmd lint"
green.outcome: The complete source-policy suite passed 57 tests and the repository lint/source scan passed after narrowing the exemption to src/theme/tokens.css.

questions: Q-0001 remains open: the owner must resolve Unicode-3.0 before the repository-wide Rust license-policy CI lane and any PR can be fully green.
Q-0004 remains open: only the visible settings-facing custom-theme import control is parked.
Q-0005 remains open: the explicit conservative contrast matrix is implemented but not claimed as the owner's final normative matrix.
Q-0006 remains open: full custom-theme CSS color syntax, normalization, compositing, and contrast acceptance are parked.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
